steps.title: Find the larger side length
steps.content: The larger map side length is $80\%$ longer than $60$ inches, so it is $180\%$ of the original side length: $$60(1+0.80)=108$$ The same actual distance is now spread across $108$ inches instead of $60$ inches.

steps.title: Keep the actual length fixed
steps.content: The original $60$-inch side represented the actual distance found by multiplying inches by miles per inch: $$60\cdot 12=720$$ On the larger map, $108$ inches represents the same $720$ miles.

steps.title: Divide by larger inches
steps.content: The actual distance represented by $1$ inch on the larger map is about $$720\div 108=6.666$$ This is closest to $6.67$. The answer is B.
